reviews.review_text:
A nice little game to play in short bursts: you build your deck and try to make it to the end of the run, constantly facing new hurdles to overcome along the way. A bit of luck is involved, of course you can't win every game.

Otherwise, the concept is excellent and offers plenty of variety; you’ll quickly find yourself wanting to play several rounds in a row. The animations are also great, definitely a solid title for the Steam Deck for gaming on the go.

Balatro is insanely addictive in the best way possible. Every run feels fresh, and pulling off wild combos is super satisfying. It’s simple to pick up but gets deep fast, which keeps you hooked.
Easily one of the best “just one more run” games

poker on STEROIDS.

if your expecting to play poker, you'll be dissapointed. 

illegal hands / fake hands, 150+ jokers that all have unique abilities, you pretty much have free reign over creating and destroying any card or upgrade you have, along with like 20+ unique decks + challenge runs + 'stake' runs with certain debuffs.

level up hands, figure out synergies between jokers, wake your family up at 3am after smashing your keyboard.

fair warning 100% takes 100s of hours if your an achievement hunter.

genuinely peak dopamine rush, 8.8/10.
I love this game but the bosses targeting your build every time makes it very frustrating and not fun.
so fun its just chaos in the version of cards and its beautiful
Best introduction to playing card games. Personally went from Balatro to solitaire as a card game progression.
My only issue is that there is no more incentive to play after 100%. I wish there was official PvP mode or at least steam workshop support. AMAZING game.

- Great rogue-lite loop. It is immensely satisfying to get synergistic combination of jokers.
- Pleasant sound design, simple and clear UI. Props to the solo dev!
a lot of fun! I thought it was poker at first but then jokers broke open a portal of chaos the likes of which I have never seen
fun math. the sound effects and wiggle animation gives me a nice brain scratch. addicting. well made game.

It's one of those games that just keeps bringing you back. Having it on mobile is a blessing and a curse. It's a blessing because I can play Balatro whenever and where ever I want. It's a curse because I can play it whenever and where ever I want. Jimbo is a great dude

So listen, I own this game on PlayStation, I own this game on mobile, AND now I own it on Steam. That’s how much I love this game. So yeah, I might be a little biased.

But if you like card games and roguelikes (roguelites? rogue-lights? whatever, you know the type), this game is absolute crack. Every run somehow turns into “okay, just one more” until suddenly it’s 2 AM and you’re calculating poker hands like a sleep-deprived casino wizard.

It’s simple enough to pick up fast, but once the synergies start stacking, your brain turns into a dopamine-powered spreadsheet. And somehow a game about poker hands becomes one of the most satisfying strategy games out there.

10/10, would buy on a fourth platform if they let me.
Addictive. Engaging. Fantastic. If you value your productivity, do not buy this game.

If I could pay 100€ to erase my memory and blind run this game for a second time I would do it instantly. This was without a doubt the best overall gaming experience of my life. Never I have felt so empy, yet so full, after finishing a game. What I felt in this journey, from the beginning to the end, is too hard to describe for me.

Of course t's not a triple A game, so it has some little flaws. I'll tell you what in the end bothered me the most: you can only play it once. Yet, it's not actually a flaw, it's how games like this work. It's how life works. And if you loved the experience as much as I and my friends who played it did, it will feel bad for days, probably weeks, maybe even more. I still think about it after almost a month.

There will of course be some people that will hate its mechanics or won't really enjoy the gameplay. You are constantly facing problems, sometimes you will feel like you are making so much progress, sometimes it will feel like you won't ever understand how to reach your goal. I think that's part of what makes the experience so beautiful, yet I am also sure many won't agree with me. It's not a game for everyone, no videogame is.

But please, if you want to give yourself a gift, buy this game, take your time, try not to read spoilers online, and experience in first person what it means to be a little gambler on his first journey to the casino, left alone to unravel the mystery of the card deck.

It will be truly worth your money and time.
This game is peak gambling and solitaire combined with special joker cards. this game is very fun and addictive, it's must play ⭐
Fun and challenging game, really exciting to see the numbers go up
addictive in the most harmless and fun way possible

Don't buy pls

Saw the memes back when Balatro came out and couldn't get how a ♥♥♥♥♥♥ poker game could be addictive (mind you I've played poker night at the inventory 1 and 2 and used to be such a blackjack degenerate that used to spend 6+ hours everyday in the gta online casino during covid). 
Bought it for 10 bucks now that it's on sale. Got the jist of it very quickly - I get it now. The different color decks and booster packs alone give it a ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ of replayability, not even mentioning jokers.
After 4-5 failed attemps I cheesed my first run with a black deck at 1.30 am w/ 6 very lucky jokers, playing only one pairs  (lvl 19), dopamine receptors going wild. 

Seriously considered downing a Monster lo-carb to stay awake all night playing and just not go to my internship. Slept 5 hours, feel like ♥♥♥♥ and went anyways. Actually regret not doing it. Crazy ♥♥♥♥
When I play a five of a kind ace flush in real poker everyone accuses me of "cheating" but in Balatro it's just normal.
It feeds my gambling addiction without spending a ton of money, 10/10 would recommend

This is a decent game, but not a game for me. The single song soundtrack that is remixed 4 different times was really great, for the first few hours. It got redundant, and a variety of music as good as the main song would have made the game better. The gameplay is a really fun and creative take on poker, but the luck that goes into each hand and round can get irritating at times, and this game never hooked me enough to want to watch YouTube videos on strategies to beat gold stake decks. The aesthetic is a vibe, and the variety and uniqueness of jokers is cool. This is an instant where I wish steam had an in between rating, but since the game is an overall fun experience, I’ll give it a positive rating.

This game is amazing. I could ramble for hours about how it's probably one of the best designed games I have ever played, and an extremely fun experience for basically anybody, featuring excellent, fun & diverse strategies and synergies, and immense stakes with hard limits on how much you can do in each blind meaning every move feels intentional & tense, because the game doesn't tell you quite how much you'll score until you play your hand & pray it's enough to beat the blind on your final turn.

Really amazing game, an incredible amount of content that will last you hours upon hours with you constantly discovering new content throughout, and just an amazing, fun, accessible, well designed game overall.
haven't played deck type games before, but ever since I started playing I can't stop
Bro if you dont have the game get it right now or else i will turn you into a red seal steel king....... im warning you

amazing game! even got a little too hooked on it for a bit there – the completionist grind offers essentially hundreds of hours of gameplay, but now that i'm a bit distanced from my balatro era, i don't really feel any incentive whatsoever to come back and continue playing. but the gameplay loop is super fun, decks offer decent variety, there are tons of jokers, mod support, so many great things.

Do not buy this game because you will become addicted and spend well over 50 hours on it. It's been one of the most fun achievement hunting experiences I've ever had. Any bugs feel like features so there really isn't any problem with the game at all. Well maybe besides blue stake which is getting addressed in the next update. The developer localthunk, is the GOAT. Thank you Balatro.
great game (very addicting) i highly reccomend to all but, if you have a problem with getting un addicted with things i dont think its the best idea unless you have alot of time.
This is truly one of the greatest games I've ever played. As a trade-off for the amazing experience this game provides, it eats up a chunk of your soul; would recommend.
The game is too addictive, and not really rewarding. The RNG is way more important than strategy. If you want a time-sink, go ahead.
I don't usually get addicted to games long term but this is one of those few exceptions for me
poker if it was like smoking crack, taking adderall, and jerking off for 24 hours until you die
This game is so dopemein rushing and very very cool, I love it so much

Balatro is simple, cheap, and fun. In short, you play poker hands with some twists. You are the only person playing, and your hand is scored based on criteria you manipulate. It hits all your dopamine receptors in the same way as gambling, but only charges you $15 one time. I recommend it for casual and hard core gamers and anyone who likes seeing numbers go up.

I think it is a very fun game, especially when you already know the poker hands. The jokers are also a really cool extra feature that isn't in most other poker games I know. I highly recommend this game!

Number go up!
Nice game for some sessions, quite a cool game loop.

The premise is so simple, yet the game is incredibly deep and engaging. I found that I would progress to a certain level of skill and understanding, then hit something of a wall, and I would need to rethink my approach or seek advice from experts to pick up a new trick or two to keep moving forward. Usually once I did, I was able to start making more of my own observations and strategies. This was all a very satisfying cycle of learning and progress. 

The Completionist trophies ensure that you will not run out of things to do for a very long time. 

Balatro feels like a museum-worthy work of art in the field of game design.

Everyone knows this game, it's the TikTok of the roguelite genre, the easy dopamine game. Try it out.

This review is here to warn you that Balatro can RANDOMLY ERASE ALL YOUR STAKE PROGRESS so please BACK UP YOUR SAVE FILES! I lost gold stake wins on all decks, man... (the game didn't award me with the Completionist+ achievement either)

Didn't know the game until I saw a video on YouTube. The video got me excited, looked fun & I liked the premise. So I pulled the trigger. Simply said, I was hooked! I even bought the mobile version to further fuel my love for this game. 28 hours on Steam & God know how man on mobile, this game still keep reeling me in to play another session! If you're hesitant, don't! Just buy it!

I beat a round on my 4th try. Balatro is a fun time especially if you set your jokers correctly you can freeze the game for a moment for it to check it's calculations if you think carefully.

Even more fun if you mod the game to allow Multiplayer to pit yourself against other players on the same seed to see if anyone is better then you!

Seriously a fun time killer if you are bored.

https://www.backloggery.com/Zeroman95BL
Very fun strategy with the various ways you can mix and match Jokers! I love the planet cards which level up the different hands too.
As if poker wasn't addictive enough on  its own good lord. This game is fantastic, super intuitive and user friendly. A real hoot. Watch the clock.
The kind of game you say "one more round" to. Really fun gameplay; expect to get addicted!
My favourite game in recent years, so addictive I have it on Xbox Playstation, mobile and steam!

♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥, this is an amazing game. Indie devs for the win once more, but I do have one complaint. 150 jokers sounds like a lot, but in reality, it doesn’t feel like it. I think until this game gets another update, it’s pretty boring after 60 hours and Completionist++. Mods will heavily improve your experience, but you should play the base game first. LocalThunk did a great job with balancing.
so good it spawned its own sub-genre. seriously, this is one of those one in a million indie games that hits GOOD.

I don't know what I can say about this game that like 100 other reviews aren't already saying, so if you haven't picked up Balatro, what are you doing? Just get it, it's like $15 and I've had so much more fun with this game than most $70 games now

This is an excellent rougelike with a deckbuilding component.  It has huge variation and you'll consequently get hugely different outcomes for a long time, and eventually, figure out enough about the mechanics that your first lucky run really comes together, you get all the breaks, and you beat it all the way through.
very very fun although i wish it had cross saves so i could buy the other versions and play on the go
I've played enough hours on this game to say its really fun and the combinations are practically endless. The modded side of the community is also really fun.
This is the greatest game. No one will top this so they should just stop making them.

Fun Game but does lean a little to far into gambling for my taste. you can win with almost any run if you know what you are doing but some times things will go wrong that are out of your control and it feels horrible. Still one of the best games for having big number go bigger with very satisfying pings though

I played this game for hundreds of hours on mobile before I even had a pc and it has never once gotten old. And since then, I've played many roguelike deckbuilding games and this one is still better than any of them. It's relatively fast paced, and despite the game's simplicity, every run presents you with unique challenges, so you're never bored. It's also extraordinarily well balanced, you can find a case to use every joker, and even the worst ones can become your greatest assets if you build your deck properly. Even just thinking about building my deck around a niche joker gets me hyped up it's so much fun. This is genuinely in my top 3 favorite games of all time. I love Balatro and I think that everyone should play it.
Game is fun enough, though I play it more on mobile than I do on the computer at this point. It runs on basically anything you throw it at. Would highly recomment.
Very addicting and satisfying games. The best part is to find different variations of jokers, building your deck and finally absolutely destroying the mechanics and winning several rounds.
It's like gambling, I tried, won, liked it, started to lose but believed in best, lost a huge amount of time but won big stakes, and still play. 10/10 gambling addictions
incredible game if you like roguelikes and/or card games. Pulls you in.

LET'S GO GAMBLING!

Such a fun and addictive game, cannot recommend it enough.
This game is full of depth that I have not seen in any other game. The amount of synergies, the satisfying plinks, and the high scores make this game what it is- Awesome. (and Addicting).
So fun I actually bought it for my phone as well even though the accounts are separate lol

Addictive game. There's a lot of fun builds you can make in your deck to prioritize whatever kind of style you want to go for. With some careful planning and luck, you can get game-breaking scores in the quadrillions by playing a single card.

Great soundtrack. Runs smooth on a toaster.

Card game... and its good! Think of poker, but with dice rules and otherworldly and planetary powers at your disposal. Definite buy if you fw jokers, jesters, bananas, blueprints, hearts, spades, clubs, diamonds, wild cards, planets, tarot, photographs, and wrecking the economy. Plant shivers when Chicot loses his temper. ♥♥♥♥ *belisk.

This game is a masterpiece. There isn't one build or one joker that is better than all the others; it's all about using what the game gives you. Even the "bad" jokers have their niche uses. The excitement of finding something good and the agony of losing (whether it's completely on accident or a bad draw) and throwing it all away are what makes it so addicting.

An absolute masterpiece when it comes to game design, replayability, creative freedom addictiveness and challenge. but there are 2 main places it falls short notably the North face of Mount Everest ahh learning curve and that as you come to truly appreciate the game, you realise that reaching the true heights of this brilliant game requires both skill and unfortunately dumb luck. Yet, i have loved 100%ing this game, and loved the community and don't regret buying it. all in all, i'd recommend this game but just know what you are getting into ;)

Personal GOTY for 2024 until GTA 6 release and maybe even after. Some games are piss, others are your nice average cup of coffee, once in a while a game like a fine wine gets released and then there's this. Your absolute mother of all opioids packed in a retro looking game. I bought this game on two different devices because when I want to stop playing, I do it because I either want to have a change of screen. It's not enough to be addicted to this game, because if you do stop, you're going to watch others play better than you on Youtube or Twitch. I recommend this game, but be cautious because it might just consume all your time and your thoughts.
This is my favorite game OAT no dif and I've played a lotta games. most of my hours are on mobile but this is truly a masterpiece W game
Good game but Selling Out to Mega hedge funds is bad for gaming and fear will degrade this game.

Balatro is a poker roguelike. And that works shockingly well. Using jokers with special conditions to modify how your deck scores or plays, astrology cards to improve the point scoring of hands played, and tarot cards to change the nature of cards you simply try to beat 8 antes consisting of up to three rounds.

It's simple, but due to the number of potential combinations, arguably infinitely complex. It plays well, it has a distinct visual style, it's just overall a good game.

But I feel that it's not deeply replayable, even with new challenges or difficulty levels. Ultimately you're trying to generate chips (points) and multipliers, so there's only one real build and just a question of how you get there. You can change decks, and focus on different hands, but since only a few hands have enough scaling value, at a certain point it's all pretty much the same. The reality of variation and randomness is just whether you got something usable in your first two antes, if so great, you've got a winning run. If not, start again.

As a result, it gets to be a very monotonous game, with very little joy of discovery. But that is AFTER you've had a genuinely good time for a while.

[h1]8/10[/h1]

Full disclosure, a lot of the time I've put into this game [i]has[/i] been modded. That doesn't take away the joy of just playing this game vanilla.

This game is one of the truest forms of "just one more run". Things can go so right so quickly that if you [i]do[/i] attempt one more run, you could just fly through it. If things don't go that way, though, you could have a long and arduous uphill battle. Either way it happens, though, it's still a joyous time that I've happily sunk a hundred hours into, and will happily sink hundreds more.
Its Balatro... It was Best Indie Game of the Year. That was 2 years ago, its still peak. Its still Goated. And I'll be damned before I see this game fall.

What is a seemingly simple game is anything but. Balatro takes poker and turns it into something so much more. The endless combinations of joker cards and strategies keep the replayability high. I've sunk 40+ hours into this game on PC and even play on my phone and still continue to come up with new strategies and combinations. Be warned, this is a very addicting game. You will dream about it. You will think about it while at work. It is just that good.
Makes the brain tick just right. The multiplayer mod makes it a ton of fun with friends!
EXTREMELY addictive, complex and never-ending
I reallt like deckbuilding/roguelike mechanics and love this game, it's very replayable and scalable, love the art and the soundtrack.

It's peak. Amazing gameplay loop. Amazing theme.
The game does what its trying to do perfectly.
Many different decks offer flavourful ways to play the game on high difficulty. Endless allows for high number chasing including different ways to break the games score limit. 
Even more replayability since Multiplayer mods are now a thing.
extremely fun and addicting
Such a great game satisfying and endless would recommend for everyone

Balatro is a one-of-a-kind game that you cannot overlook. If you're into roguelites, card games or simply just a fan of big numbers that activate your neurons, then you need to try this game out. It's simple to pick up and understand after a few games, before you realize that there's so much more to it that you've still yet to understand. Even 100 hours later, I still find myself discovering new things about the game and new ways to play. It's amazing, I can't describe it any other way, and you need to try it out yourself to understand it as well.
this game puts me halfway between jumping for joy and committing vehicular manslaughter. Don't balatro and drive, 10/10
This game will lead you to either enlightenment or insanity, perhaps even both at the same time.
A fabulous yet overrated game that's style leaves a lasting impression. The game boils down to hoping for the right jokers to show up, which I don't find terribly fun or clever.

Amazingly fun rogue-like that uses poker with crazy jokers that throw a simple card game into an addictive "one-more-round" addiction. Tons of fun to play with your significant other and switch out runs. Each deck is unique with challenges able to be added as you progress high and higher in difficulty. 200 hours in and I'm about half way through the challenges. Thank you for reading my review. Happy gaming!

Addicting. Music/audio design is amazing. Love how you can put the game in higher speeds too.

Totally worth if you're in a lecture or wanting to find a game you can play while watching something else or doing something else. My favorite casual experience ever!
Pure soul. Haters and emulators can't hock it.

Okay, at first I was like, "I don't see what all the fuss is about." But then I couldn't stop playing. I'm still only a baby player but it is a blast. Love finding new cards to play, new combinations. It feels a lot like a slot machine when you hit bonus on bonus on bonus. Definite recommend for those who are looking for a fun AF time waster!!!

Hands down, one of the best roguelites you can buy. This game opened my eyes to the countless other games like it, and has led me to eventually build my own game taking a heavy inspiration from it (Coming out in the next few months!). Although there are better roguelikes such as Nuclear Throne, this game holds a place in my list of the best in the genre released in the last few years. Not much else to say. BUY THE GAME.
Interesting game, do not recommend, made me addicted to gambling
One of the best rouge-lite experiences in card form. Truly a joy to play.

At first, I tought it could be boring, but it's one of the best card games I've ever played it feels like going to the casino without spending real money, its a very simple relaxing game that you can play for hours!

I put off buying this game because I thought I'd play it way too much. I was right. If you like rougelights and/or deckbuilders, I'd say that this game is very much worth it. It also goes on sale semi frequently.
Super engaging loop - kept me glued. Devs learn from this game of how to make a game actually just plain old fun.
A very good game. I recomend it for rougelike lovers and anyone who likes watching numbers grow. Also, it has a very fun modding scene.

After 240 hours, I have finally gotten C++ and I can say this is an excellent game. It's a good time waster, depression stopper, and there is a thriving community. Absolutely my favorite game since I was a child.

I put in hundreds of hours on this game on a naval deployment using the steam deck, which runs beautifully and easily on it. This game was my first step into the genre and it blew me away sky high. Couldn't recommend this game more.
One of the most brilliant roguelikes ever made. A true masterpiece that every fan of the genre must play.

Addictive and fun gameplay with a LOT of replayability. Its the motivative kind of challenging that has TONS of collectables. Plus if you want to skip to the good decks and difficallies, theres an "unlock all" opition for creating a profile. Overall a true classic for anyone new to steam
Was never into roguelikes like that but I can see why this game was nominated for GOTY. Everything from the visuals to the sound and gameplay keeps me coming back. Looking forward to getting C++

OMG yes, this game is for everyone. It has over 100,000 reviews on App Store, and still a perfect 5.0 rating, and here's why. I'm not that big a fan of gambling, and I didn't know how to play poker at all, and yet still found myself playing 10 hours in my first day. I beat Ante 8, insanely satisfying, and still more to do, tons. Crazy how this game was made by literally one dude.

Frustratingly addictive game. This game has a difficulty that seems not only reachable but very unforgiving if not careful with your choices. If you enjoy games with randomized aspects that make or break a run, this game is for you.
Scratches a similar itch to Hearthstone Battlegrounds, which is great, because that's exactly what I wanted from it.
